Hello friends and welcome to our playbook called the webinar registration rev up. This is part of Ease 2 and if you are running webinars or considering running webinars or trying to be convinced to run webinars, this is a playbook that you'll definitely want to pay attention to and take the time to implement. But before we begin, I'm going to open up with a question. It's not rhetorical. It's one for you to actually evaluate and consider on your own. But when it does come to webinars, would you rather have a room of 50 folks, perhaps 50 people that you actually know by name because it's not such a bloated list and convert maybe 30 to 40% of them or would you rather have 200, a really big list and convert 8 to 10%. They roughly come out to about the same revenue, right? Profit on the other hand depends on where those 200 leads are coming from versus those 50. Leads aren't getting cheaper. Webinar registrations aren't getting cheaper. And it stands to reason that we need to make the most out of what we got. We need to favor intimacy and higher conversion rates than converting based off of blunt force of numbers alone. So this is where we're coming from with these AI playbooks around webinars. It's about maximizing what we got. It's about creating richer, more dynamic, more intimate experiences and converting higher percentages because of those playbooks versus making our launches work through blunt force. Just putting enough volume through a funnel and accepting what we got get out of it. So that's what we're going to be up to here. The big reframe, you know, and I think this is what is most realistic to a lot of online businesses right now is that unless you do have a major list of 20,000 plus subscribers that you've been building, nurturing and selling to over the last few years, then webinars are not actually a one too many sales channel. That's kind of the big deception around webinars that they are this one too many leveraged way of selling. And yes, they are more leveraged, but they're not a one too many sales channel. There's still one to one, just in a more leveraged way. And we're going to show you what we mean by that in just a second. Instead I want you to consider though that there are only one leveraged aspect of an intimate one to one sales conversation. So sorry, not sorry, maybe this is amazing news. If you do enjoy that interaction, if you do enjoy connecting deeply with your pre customers and having a role in that conversion in a one to one kind of way, or maybe this is awful news to you if you just wanted to kind of run your webinar and whoever joins based on your slide deck presentation and your pitch joins and everyone else kind of just falls off and you live to fight another day. That's not what we're going to be going for here. We're going to go for a bit of a change up on the traditional model. And I believe with good reason and you can choose to agree or disagree based on what we're going to talk about here. So your current webinar model, one that you're likely familiar with is where you would have you know your webinar registration perhaps that's ads driving to it. Maybe that's your Facebook group driving to it. Maybe it's your list driving to it. Regardless, you'll start with that webinar registration on the landing page. So we'll be a confirmation video. Perhaps you'll send out some show up emails, a confirmation email, some reminders before the webinar starts, try to encourage the show up. Then a certain percentage will show up on the webinar itself. A certain percentage will stay. Those who don't buy will get your follow up sales email sequence on a four, five, six, seven day sequence. And that's kind of it. That's the strategy that is like the nuts and bolts of running a webinar and there's nothing wrong with it. It's a great starting point. It's a necessary starting point. All these pieces are still valid. However, if we're being really honest about it and what else are we doing if we're not being honest about it, the level of intimacy there is low to medium. It's still extremely leveraged. The only intimacy you're really having within this is maybe the webinar Q&A. And how intimate is just seeing someone's name on a Zoom chat and then answering them directly. It has some degree of intimacy. It's not to be discounted. It's awesome. But it kind of maxes out on the level of intimacy. And that's fine. Once again, if you're getting the results you desire and the results that feel good and work for your business. But what we're typically seeing with this setup right now, you know, in 2021, as this is being recorded is show up rates, depending on industry, of course, will vary between 10 to 18%. This used to be closer to 25 to 30 back in 2014. It's only shrinking. It's only going down. And your close rates on the webinar itself of those who stayed till the end will usually be somewhere between 7 to 10%. Of course, that is a general benchmark. Yours could be higher, yours could be lower, but that's a good rule of thumb. Now, what we're attempting to do with the AI webinar model is change things up just a little bit. You don't have those core pieces. You know, there's still going to be the webinar registration. But what if, in addition to the confirmation video on the landing page, there was actually a personalized welcome video? Right? What if you actually reached out to those on your registration list, those 50 people and created deeper engagement and relationship building with them before they even get on the webinar. So that's what we're going to be focusing on in this playbook right here as part of Ace Number Two. And then yes, show up emails. Of course, that's going to be there. Customized webinar presentation, what I mean by that is if in that personalized welcome video, you kind of open it up to them, say, hey, I'm just actually finishing the slides for this right now and want to know if you had any specific questions about this topic that you'd like me to include before I wrap it up. So you are creating those reach outs and you're actually integrating some of those questions and topics straight into your presentation. So it becomes more relevant to your audience, right? And relevancy is something we spoke about earlier in this program. Sales emails plus individual follow-ups. So we're not only relying on this one to many very static asset like sales emails. If we go back to touch point theory, we always want to ascend it, right? If someone actually got on your webinar, we don't want to downgrade their experience of intimacy. We want to ascend it, right? With some individual follow-ups. So what if someone stayed till the end, right? They sat through your content, the value you were giving and ultimately through your offer, right? This is someone who has invested time, energy, emotion into your offer and to visualizing themselves partaking in it. And it's time for them to kind of make a decision about it, right? Now what if instead of just letting our sales emails try to finish the job, sort of speak, we actually took that initiative, right? To download that list of the 20, 30, 40 people that stayed till the end. Perhaps not a massive list. I'm not sure. But what if we actually took that list and we created an individual experience and just said, hey, awesome of you to stay till the end of our presentation. I know we covered a lot. I just want to make sure that you had everything you need to make a decision about this program, right? So nothing high-pressure, just something that would acknowledge their situation and ascend the intimacy instead of downgrading it at that point in your funnel. So when you take these additions into account, we upgrade the intimacy of the current webinar model from low to medium to medium to high, right? Where they have this option and this opportunity to engage with you directly in their decision-making process. Now, what else are we trying to achieve here, right? The show-up rates, bumping those from 10 to 18, working to double that to 30 to 40%. This has been true in our experience based on sending these personal customized, sorry, these personal videos upon registration, right? Because you send that over and they're no longer anonymous. You're acknowledging them. You're seeing them. You're expecting them. There's a bit of a social contract there, right? That you're excited for them to be there. You're going to actually create aspects of your webinar based on what they need most. So it's less anonymous. It's less distant of a process and you're going to get a higher show-up rate because of that. Some more on that in a second. And the goal, of course, of all of this is for higher conversion rates on that offer, right? Between these individual touchpoints, between coaching decisiveness in an intimate way, we are looking to get that close rate closer to 25 to 40%, which as well in our experience implementing these playbooks in our own launches has fallen within that range. Cool. So to be clear, I've had a lot of success with the old model, you know, working with companies like Amy Porterfield, working with copy hackers. But we have to kind of acknowledge that these are well-established brands that have been building their list over the last 10 years quite literally and still, still would greatly benefit from these AI optimizations. Of course, it's different at that level of scale. You certainly need some degree of support and to empower someone on your customer success team or your marketing team to be able to take ownership of these reach-outs. But these optimizations at every degree of scale I would make the argument are becoming a high ROI task, right? When you're fighting for those show-up rates, when you're fighting for those conversions and fighting is the wrong word, it should feel effortless and fun and it should feel based on these coaching principles that we're learning. But unless you are working with a brand with a blunt force ability to just push so much volume that you still get results, we do need to look at every opportunity to increase the conversion potential of our webinar funnel. So that's the invitation. If you're not yet at that level where you can win on volume alone, well then we do need to attempt to punch above your weight class and we do this by getting more registrants to actually commit to that enrollment conversation and joining and actually like making it to the webinar. We have to do so by getting more pre-customers to actually stay on the webinar, right? By not having them duck off the moment there's kind of this idea of a pitch, right? And we start by being transparent right off the bat that there will be an opportunity to join your program. It's going to be an amazing opportunity, probably the best one you've ever put together. But that's there, right? And we get them to commit and feel safe and feel comfortable staying till the end where the actual offer is delivered and they have a chance to enroll. And then finally, converting more of those pre-customer attendees into empowered, informed enrollment. So these are the three things we're really looking at when we talk about punching above your weight class and the playbooks that you're going to learn here and in ACE number three are in service to precisely that. So part one, which you're learning right here, is all about the pre-webinar automated intimacy actions you're taking and part two in ACE three is going to be about the closing phase post-webinar. So what to do with those attendees who stayed till the end, heard your offer, but didn't make a decision on the webinar itself. That is one of the highest intent buyer ready segments that you'll ever have in your marketing and one where there is certainly very good reason and very high ROI in adding a dynamic element to your marketing. Cool. So let's begin. What exactly can you do in the minutes or hours immediately after someone signs up for your webinar? So this, as we learned, is a moment of heightened receptivity where they just took a milestone action that has demonstrated high intent and motivation, especially when ads, emails, landing pages, posts are transparent about the impending offer. This is where I highly, highly recommend that every asset you have that points to a webinar registration, whether that's in your emails, in your ads, on your landing page, in your confirmation video, in your personalized video, are fully open and proud and excited that there's going to be an offer. It's not something you've got to apologize about. It's an opportunity that you need to assume based on them registering would be excited to receive. And when you know that every registrant is aware that an offer is coming, is excited about an offer, you're going to feel that much more confident moving into the pitch phase and the offer walkthrough part of your webinar. So this is such a crucial aspect that I feel doesn't get utilized enough. A lot of people still feel like, you know, icky being transparent about that and then even ickier as their audience feels a little bait and switched on the webinar itself. There's no reason for that, right? Be transparent up front so that way you know that everyone who registers is aware of it and excited to hear what you have to say and what you have on offer. So this is that moment of heightened receptivity that sign up me and Ty and Tint and Motivation, especially when you're talking about, you know, there being an offer, it signals proximity to purchase, right? If someone registers for that webinar where they know an offer is going to be there, they're quite close to that buying line. Then of course it signals willingness to invest time and energy, right? They just signed up to spend 50, 55 minutes from you, right? And maybe in some industries it's for the sheer sake of learning something. But in marketing industries, you know, in B2B industries, usually someone who's investing that time out of their day, yes, they want to learn something, but they also want to learn how to go deeper and get a more complete solution or have a more complete way of working with you to get the fullest expression of the results. So that's why webinars are so powerful in my view. They signal these very important precursors to that final conversion and it's why it's worth us investing back in our pre-customers as much as possible here. So in short, what we want to do immediately upon that sign up is remind them of this, remind them of all these things that are likely going on, right? And mirror back the commitment they've just made. So just like in ace number one, when we spoke about adding meaning to the click in the form of, you know, the initial opt-in, we have to make sure that our pre-customer knows, feels, and connects to the meaning of the webinar registration, the bigness of it, the importance of it, the monumental nature of it while not allowing complacency to set in, right? So what we want to do is we want to make sure that we have the right sense of, oh, this was a really big, exciting action I took in the time when I was feeling particularly motivated. I had the right song playing on Spotify and I saw your ad and everything felt good and I did it. And now it's two days later and I just don't give a shit, right? Too much has happened. The moment is too diluted. You know, my kid is being a little jerk, whatever it is, right? We don't want to allow complacency to set in. The tenderness and excitement that they felt upon the initial opt-in and try to carry that over as much as possible until the webinar itself. So two core actions we're going to be doing in service of this. We're going to send them a confirmation email. So nothing too crazy or fancy here, but we're going to give you the webinar edition of the What This Says About You email that we spoke about in ace number one. And then we're going to activate the playbook of sending a personalized reach-out via whatever video reach-out tool you're using or even personal email or messenger. So that's what we're going to be doing. Action number one, the confirmation email. There should be a template pack somewhere below this video that you can kind of review and look through and use as motivation. But with y'all who are likely more advanced marketers and copywriters and experienced in using your own voice. I've wanted to air a little bit away from suggesting exact templates, right? Even if these weren't swipe and deploy, madlib style, I wanted to give you a little bit more flexibility and give you just enough constraint in order to make sure you're hitting the right beats. So with that said, five core elements that you'll want to include, you want to honor and celebrate the click. Just celebrate them for doing this. Tell them how big of a deal it is, right? And mean it. And we're going to be doing this to create a degree of magnetism of celebrating and honoring them. Next, be a human. You know, tell fun short stories where possible and let them feel your excitement for them, right? This is so vital. Be someone that they actually want to hang out with for an hour, right? That's an investment of time. That's an investment of energy. Some people light us up when we spend time with them. Some people drain us, right? In an email, you could tell on a subconscious level if the person you're going to be spending time with is going to elevate you and excite you and energize you or if it's going to be a bit more of an energy drain. So that's what you're going to be doing there. You also want to remind them of the tangible benefits and outcomes they signed up for, right? And talk about, you know, what you're committed to helping them experience as well as what you'll be teaching in service of that, right? So obviously you are promising certain outcomes, promising certain things you're going to be teaching on your landing page, on the webinar, on the, sorry, the ads driving to the webinar, the emails driving to it, whatever got them to that signup page, it was for a certain outcome that they got excited about. So this is where you want to remind them that those things are on the way, that you're going to be excited and committed to helping them experience it. Next, sell the show up, right? This is so vital. You know, it's sad that some people still think that like webinar registration, you know, is the end of the journey here, right? I mean, 15%, you know, on the high end, which is typical right now is pretty abysmal, right? And bumping it to 30 can be game changing. Even 30 sounds way lower than it should be. But we have to be so active in this phase, right? Of reinforcing that commitment of selling the show up and using whatever elements we have decided are, you know, viable and relevant to us and our business for doing so. My favorite way to do it, right, is be transparent about the offer itself, right? So this goes hand in hand with what we spoke about just a few seconds ago, right? I'm proud of the offer, right? I feel so good selling the show up by saying you'll get access to the founders edition of AI, right? And this offer is not going to be available anywhere else, right? We're offering this whole package for the first time. I think you're going to love it based on what I know about you, what I've seen you're up to, right? And this is why I'm excited for you to show up live to see the offer. And if you can create an open loop around the offer itself, that's going to be one of the most powerful open loops in your business, right? Where people show up to your webinar because they're excited about the offer you've put together. That is almost the ultimate achievement you want to be able to achieve in your webinar marketing. And it's totally doable. And it all starts with creating an epic offer that actually connects to the needs and desires of your audience, as well as you personally being proud and stoked and excited and wanting people to get access to this, right? Where you could actually be proud in your emails to say, like, this is on the way, you know, and if you stay till the end, you're going to get early access to that and a chance to lock down an incredible deal. And that's something you should be able to say with a degree of pride and excitement for your audience. Cool. Bonus element. So this is something that I've tested across a few launches right now, some really big ones, some more modest ones. But proposing many actions that solidify commitment and generate momentum towards what I call a point of no return. So typically you will see the many actions of put this webinar on your calendar, right? And download the workbook, maybe print it out and bring it to the webinar. And that's fine. It creates some kind of tangible commitment, right? You're printing something out, you're bringing it into your physical reality. It's on your calendar, you're going to get the reminders. All that is great. But when I talk about many actions that generate momentum towards a point of no return, I'm talking about actions that are actually part of what you teach, part of your program, right? Where they've taken a few steps towards getting the final outcome. So it's very relevant to that dream state, to that desire, to that outcome they want to achieve. It's not a disconnected micro-action like printing something out and putting something on your calendar. So examples of this. Amy's Digital Course Academy, right? On the show-up email, on the confirmation email. I believe we gave a worksheet, right? On naming your course and how powerful would it be, right? If everyone who shows up to the webinar, right, instead of saying where they're from in the first five minutes, right? As we're getting started, as people are rolling in, what if they actually shared the name of their course? That they took the time to think of, that they took a time to work through before the webinar actually starts, right? How cool would that be if they've actually named the thing, created an emotional tie to the thing, envisioned it coming to fruition with an actual name, and then the course itself is something that would help them bridge that into reality, right? So things like that, that create an actual investment in the result, an investment in the identity of someone who would be in a post-purchase state. So that was a very powerful tool we used in Amy's launches, copy school, same thing, right? We did a lot of live writing webinars. One thing I believe we did was like, we're going to teach this sales email template, right? Now, before we get started, if you really want to get a head start, schedule it, right? Open up your convert kit, open it up, open up your active campaign, create a draft, right? And schedule that draft to go out on X-Date, right? Three days after our webinar. So that action is already taken, right? And then of course on the webinar, we'll teach you how to write the email. And if you join us in the full program, we'll teach you how to finalize it. And you'll have every opportunity to get feedback in our office hours, right? In our coaching calls, in our communities. So that mini-action is actually generating momentum towards that dream state. It's not a disconnected thing. Some other quick examples, if you're doing a sewing masterclass, right? And you're going to teach how to sew a certain pattern or a certain toque or whatever it is, right? Now, before the webinar starts, what if the pre-webinar homework was buy this pattern from Amazon Prime with 24-hour shipping, right? So they have skin in the game. They've actually bought something and don't want that to be a sunk cost, right? There's a sunk cost now to not going on your webinar and learning how to use it. And there's perhaps a sunk cost in not joining your membership where they learn all the different things they can do with that pattern. Automated intimacy, you know, we could say on one of our webinars, create these tags in your CRM in advance, right? And on the webinar or in our program, we'll teach you how to actually get the best and fullest use out of it. Quiz funnel, think of your three main buckets in advance. Bring that to the table before it starts. OBM mastery, right? Create a commitment and momentum towards that ideal state, towards that identity, right? Maybe the homework there is change your LinkedIn job description to reflect your new roles and capacities that you're going to be able to get at the end of this program, right? And you've already created an identification with that end state. You've created momentum, emotional investment. And then when the webinar comes, you finally get a chance to make good and bridge that full gap. So these are things to consider. If you do want a swipe pack of some of these webinar confirmation emails we're referring to and that we've used in the past, there should be a link or a doc somewhere right below this video that you guys can check out. Okay, so big action number two will be the personalized video reach out. So what we're doing here is we're coaching responsibility, right? By creating a two-way dialogue. But most importantly, acknowledging their action personally and directly, sorry, we're coaching mastery by mentioning very quickly some of the things that you're excited to teach, some things that are very unique to you and your IP and your method and your teachings. And we're coaching resonance and magnetism around you personally, right? By mere fact of you investing the time and energy in this reach out, right? In this relationship, especially when they see you as a leader or a coach to hundreds of people, right? And you're taking this 30 seconds right now to address them directly. You're creating massive resonance and magnetism just off that alone. Next, you're coaching momentum by reminding them of any of those actions or pre-work momentum builders that they can build. So based off of what we just spoke about a few seconds ago, you're coaching accountability by asking them to personally tell you if they can't make it, right? Someone is far less likely to ghost when they know they're not just a number in a funnel, an anonymous number in a funnel, but actually have someone expecting them to show up, right? So address them directly like, hey, by the way, I can't wait to see you there if for whatever reason you can't make it, if you could just let me know, right? That's common courtesy so that I can mark you off the list and open it up for someone else. Very simple, a very normal, okay expectation to set, and one that will definitely boost your show up rates. Then finally, you get to gather that voice of prospect data, right? Around their intent for joining, gathering any questions that they have around the topic, which you can actually integrate as we spoke about earlier into your webinar content itself, right? And then address that person as you teach that content on your webinar. Say, Sarah brought up an amazing question about this, and I just want to make sure that we integrated into this and that this is clear for everyone else. So you could actually have banked Q&A queues, you could have topics to discuss. Bank Q&A queues are awesome, right? So that when the webinar ends and you say, does anyone have any questions? And no one really feels safe going first, especially if you have a list full of introverts. You could just draw on this bank, right? So like, while you guys think of the questions you have, like anything that's coming to mind right now, we actually have a few that came in earlier today, and we're going to start here. And you could start riffing on that without having that awkward dead space. Cool. So very important note. We're going to reiterate again, is this personal video should not feel rehearsed or scripted. So to be clear, you're better off sounding, you're better off being natural off the cuff and perhaps missing a few of these points than hitting them all, but sounding like you're essentially just reading off a script. So it should have that personal note. Here is an example of how it could look. This is a little loose script that you can use. But once again, not meant to be a script, meant to be shot and delivered off the cuff in a very natural way. So what it might sound like is, hey, Raj, just saw your name show up on our registration list for the webinar we're running tomorrow. I just want to officially welcome you and congratulate you for signing up. There's so much exciting, practical stuff that I'm stoked to share with you that I'm sure will have an amazing effect on your business. For now, I'm hoping you wouldn't mind hitting me back with other video or just a short email, letting me know what inspired you to sign up. What are you most excited to learn? And then finally, if for whatever reason you can't make it, please also let me know directly so we can open up another spot. And we're not waiting for you before kicking things off. Hope that sounds cool. Have an awesome day. And look forward to catching you tomorrow. So this last part is going to be so critical for creating that sense of accountability, what I would call fair and legitimate accountability. And that's going to have an awesome effect on your show breaks. Cool. Action number three, optional to be used with extreme discernment. But depending on if you're using a Facebook group, depending on the size of your list, you could totally celebrate webinar registrants in your Facebook group. So this is going to be relevant and applicable if you've been using your group and the posts within it as a source for webinar registrants. So this has a few benefits to it. You're going to be creating accountability through non-anonymity. So if you tagged me in your group and said, hey, Raj, I saw you sign up for your webinar, signed up for our masterclass, congrats, can't wait to see you there. All of a sudden, I'm tagged, right? There's a bit of social accountability as well as the fact that, oh, wow, you actually noticed I signed up. Now I'm actually going to like make good and show up because I know you're expecting me to be there. So that's one way to do it. And that's of course going to have a social proof effect where other members of your group will see in real time as other people are joining, right? And that will have that social proof element to it. It can create a convo and a thread of what they're most excited to learn and any questions on the topic. So it can start a lot of those discussions in your group, which could be powerful. And overall, I'd recommend that this could potentially be useful for very small lists or groups or audiences who wouldn't be put off by being tagged for something like a webinar sign up. So this isn't something we do in our group with a very sophisticated marketing audience. It would be a bit of a, what should I say, an overestimation of intent, right? To really kind of celebrate someone just for signing up for one of our webinars. But in other cases, right, maybe it is not a B2B scenario. Maybe it is some kind of nutrition group or a fitness group or a hobby class, singing lessons, guitar lessons, sewing classes, anything like that, where you have a bit of a smaller group in a smaller community. I could see that being a legitimate play. Most importantly though, what this play accomplishes, if it makes sense for your audience, is you're essentially celebrating them as a customer before money changes hands. And we spoke a little bit about that in the Observer Effect in Module 1. But public honoring and public celebration and public acknowledgement like this is usually reserved for clients who join your program. You may have seen those posts of congratulating new members into your program, right, in the Facebook group. This essentially does that for a non-monetary conversion, which is the webinar registration. So you're investing in them by celebrating them before money changes hands in a way that's typically reserved for customers that can create more of a, yeah, more of an identity of that post purchase state, right? Of, wow, if they're celebrating me for just signing up for a webinar, I wonder what it's like on the other side of it. Cool. So some non-typical webinar registration sources before we wrap up. So you'll have your typical ones, right? Maybe your emails going out to your list, maybe some ads going out. But some non-typical or lesser typical ones to consider your Facebook group, right? Posts and approval questions. See Social Singularity Part 1 if you haven't already. But you can actually get webinar registrations through your approval questions, right? If you're running a really big masterclass in two weeks or three weeks or whatever it is, change your approval question to essentially get consent for sending them an invitation via direct message. Number two, of course, direct message if you've had a personal interaction with the person around that topic already. Nothing stops you from following up and saying, by the way, like, if you asked me a question on this a few weeks ago, I just want to actually follow up and tell you that we are putting together a masterclass on this topic. That thing would be really useful and it would be awesome to see you there, right? So that's one way to do it. And then personal one-on-one reachouts to high lead scores with a relevant tag, right? So if I'm running a webinar on sales frameworks or on enrollment frameworks, and I have someone on my list, right, a very high lead score, which we're able to filter by, who has a relevant interest tag of being interested in sales, nothing stops me from exporting that list of emails and sending personal messages to those people, right? And this might sound like quite a lot of work to do in some cases. And in some cases, it actually is, right? If you are going to be promoting a higher end program, a higher end service, a mastermind, these little touch points, right, these reachouts, when people don't expect them, yet when they're hyper relevant based on experiences you've already had, it just sets the stage for a much more relatable, relevant, and resonant marketing experience. So these are a few things to keep in mind. And that's really it. So the next lessons we have, you're going to see some swipe files for those confirmation emails, as well as a few other little surprises as we go along, as we create things that are useful in support of this lesson. But the main stuff we have are the tech walkthroughs from Phil below. So check those out. Implement it certainly in your next webinar. And definitely if you are running a webinar, stay tuned, right, for ace number three, where we'll talk about how to follow up with the highest intent pre-customers in your webinar funnel, the ones who actually stayed till the end and are now trying, trying their best to make a decision and could perhaps use your help along the way. So much love and I'll catch you guys soon.
